creator: novel@FreeBSD.org
text:
I've been experimenting with that figuring out an optimal volume label considering length limitation (32) and chars that could be used (d-characters) and came up with this approach:

(19:37) novel@:/usr/src/release %> isoinfo -d -i disc1.iso 
Setting input-charset to 'UTF-8' from locale.
CD-ROM is in ISO 9660 format
System id: FreeBSD
Volume id: 10_0_STABLE_AMD64_CD
Volume set id: 
Publisher id: THE FREEBSD PROJECT.  HTTP://WWW.FREEBSD.ORG/

(19:38) novel@:/usr/src/release %> isoinfo -d -i dvd1.iso 
Setting input-charset to 'UTF-8' from locale.
CD-ROM is in ISO 9660 format
System id: FreeBSD
Volume id: 10_0_STABLE_AMD64_DVD
Volume set id: 
Publisher id: THE FREEBSD PROJECT.  HTTP://WWW.FREEBSD.ORG/

(19:39) novel@:/usr/src/release %> isoinfo -d -i bootonly.iso 
Setting input-charset to 'UTF-8' from locale.
CD-ROM is in ISO 9660 format
System id: FreeBSD
Volume id: 10_0_STABLE_AMD64_BO
Volume set id: 
Publisher id: THE FREEBSD PROJECT.  HTTP://WWW.FREEBSD.ORG/

 - "." could not be used in version name as it's not a d-character, so replaced that with "_"
 - With the current schema the maximum length we could get for something like FreeBSD 14.3.1-RELEASE powerpc DVD. For that we'd have 14_3_1_RELEASE_POWERPC_DVD, of 26 chars length, so we have some space. Probably we could even replace "BO" with "BOOT" for boot-only iso.
